items.title: US-Iran talks to begin in Switzerland as Tehran says it closed Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: The US disputed Iran's claim the waterway is shut, a move Tehran says was a response to Israeli attacks in Lebanon.

items.title: BBC sees destroyed villages in Israeli-occupied southern Lebanon
items.summary: Travelling with a humanitarian convoy, BBC's Hugo Bachega has been given rare access to a part of Lebanon under Israeli occupation.

items.title: Lebanese turtle conservationist Mona Khalil killed by Israeli strike
items.summary: Mona Khalil, who had refused to leave the beach she had spent years protecting, died after several days in hospital, an environmental group said.

items.title: Colombia's escalating, brutal internal conflict is defining its presidential election
items.summary: A left-wing senator who backs talks with armed gangs faces an outsider endorsed by Trump.

items.title: Former Olympian denies vandalising Washington Reflecting Pool after arrest
items.summary: Davey Hearn says he was simply touching the new paint at the site out of curiosity and did not remove or alter it.

items.title: Meloni tells Trump to 'focus on your own popularity' as row escalates
items.summary: The US president earlier questioned Meloni's popularity after suggesting she "begged" for a photo at G7 summit

items.title: Israeli strikes kill six people in Gaza including Al Jazeera cameraman, officials say
items.summary: The Israeli military accused Ahmed Wishah of being a "Hamas sniper operative", without providing evidence.

items.title: Zelensky returns highest Polish honour after award stripped
items.summary: Ukraine's president said his country was open to "engagement" about "difficult and painful chapters of our shared past".

items.title: France bans alcohol at music festival events under red heatwave alert
items.summary: Annual street parties attended by millions ordered to help “preserve” healthcare services in 40C (104F) heat.

items.title: US to stop funding HIV programmes in South Africa
items.summary: More than eight million South Africans are living with HIV – the highest number of any country in the world.

items.title: Australia confirms first case of H5N1 bird flu as virus reaches every continent
items.summary: Australia was previously the only continent where the H5N1 bird flu strain had not yet been found.

items.title: African and Caribbean nations call for formal apology for transatlantic slavery
items.summary: The leaders have asked for apologies from the countries that benefited from the slave trade, as well as debt relief and financial compensation.

items.title: Bolivian president declares state of emergency
items.summary: The move comes after weeks of anti-government protests that have caused a shortage of basic goods in Bolivia.

items.title: Tourist dies in Dominican Republic luxury resort fire
items.summary: Nearly 1,700 people were evacuated from the huge blaze at Viva Wyndham Dominicus Beach in Bayahibe, a popular resort town on the Caribbean coast.

items.title: James Burrows, legendary director of Cheers and Friends, dies aged 85
items.summary: Best-known as co-creator of sitcom Cheers, Burrows directed more than 1,000 episodes of other classics including the Big Bang Theory and Will and Grace.

items.title: Boy, 12, wins hearts after trying to check sick chicken into Ethiopian hospital
items.summary: Markos's beloved hen was not responding to any treatments - so he did the only thing he could think of.

items.title: Morocco captain Achraf Hakimi to stand trial for rape
items.summary: French prosecutors confirm Morocco captain Achraf Hakimi will stand trial for rape, in accusations dating back to 2023.

items.title: Zimbabwe MPs pass bill to extend president's time in power
items.summary: The proposal would extend Emmerson Mnangagwa's term by two years and scrap direct presidential elections.

items.title: Six-year-old Ebola patient taken from DR Congo hospital found and 'doing well'
items.summary: Health facilities have come under attack during the current outbreak as a result of misinformation and fear.

items.title: Mangione's lawyers reverse course on psychiatric defence in state murder trial
items.summary: The defence team reverse course a day after saying they would argue he suffered "extreme emotional disturbance".

items.title: Do it at home too, women tell Japanese fans who cleaned World Cup stadium
items.summary: Some see a double standard: Japanese men who clean in public while their wives do all the housework.

items.title: UK actress charged with importing meth worth almost A$300m into Australia
items.summary: Emaa Hussen, 34, faces life in prison for allegedly trying to smuggle 320kg of meth hidden in bags of charcoal.

items.title: Asia's richest man Ambani announces what could be India's biggest share sale
items.summary: India's largest telecom operator is expected to raise around $4bn (£3.02bn), according to media reports.

items.title: In Trump's shadow, Vance becomes face of Iran deal
items.summary: His fierce defence of the Iran plan amid mounting criticism comes as speculation intensifies about a possible 2028 presidential run.

items.title: Moscow residents complain of black rain after largest Ukrainian attack hits oil refinery

items.title: Thirty-five killed as gunmen attack Niger's biggest airport
items.summary: Niger has been fighting a militant Islamist insurgency for a decade and in January suspected jihadists attacked the same airport.

items.summary: There are moments when life in Moscow feels completely normal - Thursday morning wasn't one of them, writes Steve Rosenberg.
